confidence_meta.reason: H.4.1 (WALCL/TGA/Reserves) release lands TOMORROW (Jul 23) - within the 48h catalyst window. MOVE below 80 (no penalty); scores not cleanly split 2-2 (3 deteriorating, 1 stable).

structured_conclusion.macro_trend: Plumbing Frozen Ahead of Jul 23 H.4.1 / Oil Shock Intensifying / Real-Rate Pressure Building
structured_conclusion.likely_rotation: Oil/gold firming on the Iran/Hormuz shock; SOXX modestly outperforming SPY; bank-adjacent credit proxies (ALLY/CACC/COF) broadly softer; Korea gate still closed despite USDKRW strength
structured_conclusion.next_catalyst: H.4.1 release Jul 23 (TOMORROW, 1 day); FOMC Jul 28-29 (6 days)
structured_conclusion.korea_gate: Closed 1/3 - USDKRW pass only; EWY below SMA20 (171.23 vs 181.81); KOSPI below 8,200 (6,797.71)

bias: Plumbing remains frozen ahead of tomorrow's H.4.1 print, but the more urgent signal is oil's acceleration - up another +2.3% today to $86.27, now +15.7% since Jul 8 on the unresolved Iran/Hormuz conflict - while MOVE has climbed for three straight sessions (68.5 -> 72.7 -> 74.7) even as VIX keeps calming, a divergence worth watching. Credit proxies (ALLY, CACC, COF) softened again despite tight HY OAS, and the real 2Y proxy continues to grind higher as nominal front-end yields rise faster than breakevens - the James pain-trade signature persisting for a third session.

counterargs.thesis: Plumbing stable / risk still contained ahead of Jul 23 H.4.1
supporting: HY OAS flat and tight at 2.69%
USDKRW well below the 1,530 gate threshold
SOXX +0.73%, semis holding up relative to SPY
against: Oil +2.3% today, +15.7% since Jul 8 - Iran/Hormuz risk unresolved and accelerating
MOVE up for a 3rd straight session (68.5->72.7->74.7), nearing stress territory
ALLY/CACC/COF credit proxies broadly softer again despite calm OAS
Real 2Y proxy rising for a 3rd session as nominal front-end outpaces flat breakevens
USDJPY pushing further above the 162 BOJ threshold
counterargs.read: The plumbing itself hasn't moved and won't until tomorrow's H.4.1 print, so today's signal is really about the tension between calm equity vol (VIX down again) and rising bond vol (MOVE up for a third session) plus an oil shock that keeps compounding. If oil keeps climbing into next week and MOVE breaches 80, the 'contained' read stops holding; for now, credit spreads (HY OAS) remain the most reassuring data point.

trade_table.rationale: +15.7% since Jul 8 on the unresolved Iran/Hormuz conflict; DFII5/DFII10 (TIPS real yields) both firmed 4bps today, the clearest sign yet the shock is starting to price into rates.
SOXX +0.73% today; SOXX/SPY ratio ticked up to 0.745 from 0.741 - modest relative strength.
Gate still closed 1/3; EWY fell another -1.19% and its 20-day SMA is only drifting lower with it (181.81), so the gap isn't closing.
Real 2Y proxy rising for a 3rd straight session (1.817%->1.864%) as US02Y outpaces a flat EXPINF2YR; front-end real-rate pressure persisting into the Jul 28-29 FOMC.
CACC and COF both down again despite flat, tight HY OAS - a divergence between index-level credit and single-name proxies worth monitoring.

catalysts.note: First plumbing move since the Jul 15 vintage - TOMORROW, within 48h

catalysts.event: US-Iran conflict / Strait of Hormuz shipping risk
catalysts.note: Primary driver of oil's rise, now +15.7% since Jul 8; DFII5/DFII10 starting to react
Sources conflict on exact date (Jul 23 vs Jul 29) - confirm on next run. HBM revenue (~$6.1B est., +32% YoY) is the key line item; DRAM/AMKR/memory-chain read-through.
Key inflation print given oil's rise

plumbing.tga_delta: flat vs Jul 21 - still Jul 15 H.4.1 vintage, next release Jul 23 (TOMORROW)
plumbing.rrp_delta: +$0.245B vs Jul 20 baseline ($0.03B) on fresh Jul 21 print - still near zero
plumbing.reserves_delta: flat vs Jul 21 - same H.4.1 vintage

duration_score.classification: Real-Rate Repricing Continuing / Oil Shock Intensifying
duration_score.reason: US02Y rose again (+4.7bps to 4.304%) while EXPINF2YR stayed flat at 2.44% for a third session, pushing the real 2Y proxy up to 1.864% (from 1.817% Jul 21) - continuing the front-end real-rate-tightening signature. DFII5 (+4bps to 2.05%) and DFII10 (+4bps to 2.35%) both firmed too, showing the TIPS market itself is starting to reprice, not just the modeled 2Y proxy. Oil's continued acceleration (+2.3% today, +15.7% since Jul 8) is the dominant unscheduled catalyst and raises the risk this becomes genuine inflation reacceleration rather than pure term-premium drift.

james_framework.what_market_IS_saying: Front-end staying sticky/rising into the Jul 23 H.4.1 print and Jul 28-29 FOMC, while an unresolved oil shock (Iran/Hormuz) adds a second, independent inflationary force.
james_framework.pain_trade: Real-rate pressure via a rising nominal front end against flat breakevens - not yet CPI panic, but oil is the wildcard that could flip this.
james_framework.watch_signal: If nominal 2Y starts falling even as oil stays elevated, that signals a growth-scare/cuts narrative overriding the inflation risk; if T5YIE/T10YIE start rising in tandem with oil, that confirms a shift toward genuine inflation reacceleration.

breakevens.breakeven_curve_signal: Breakevens still broadly calm in absolute terms, but DFII5/DFII10 (market-based TIPS) both firmed 4bps today alongside oil's continued rise - the clearest sign yet that the TIPS market is starting to price part of the oil shock.
